human_verification.notes: Reviewed event scheduling outputs for boundary discipline, booking and ticketing specificity, accessible inventory and fee handling, capacity-tradeoff handling, and safety and fraud escalation posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to recommendation-only event scheduling, prerequisite validation, capacity and sequence analysis, booking-readiness review, and escalation packaging.
Authoritative sources cover event workflow planning, accessible ticketing, fee disclosure, payment mismatch handling, anti-bot and fraud posture, and crowd-safety readiness with refresh discipline.
Research scenario families confirm booking and payment mismatch handling, refund-exception boundaries, safety and fraud escalation, capacity shortfall, tie-break conflict, and final-approval refusal.

Evaluation scenario set covers missing artifacts, capacity overload, conflicting priorities, refund and fraud boundaries, high-volume intake, terminology normalization, policy conflicts, and tenant adaptation.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across ticketing, pricing, refund, fraud, security, venue, and approval-refusal cases.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
